task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent opening the 'world_survey.gpkg' file in a GIS application (like QField) or connecting to it via a database terminal.

task_completion.description: Evaluate the proximity of Kinshasa, Nairobi, and Addis Ababa to the equator
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent checking the coordinates, measuring distances, or panning the map to inspect Kinshasa, Nairobi, and Addis Ababa relative to the equator (latitude 0).

task_completion.description: Identify and focus on Nairobi as the target city
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent navigating to, zooming in on, or querying Nairobi, Kenya, having identified it as the closest city to the equator.
Visual evidence of the agent selecting or toggling edit mode for the 'field_observations' layer in the GIS app, or specifying this table in a SQL command.

task_completion.description: Add a point feature at Nairobi's location
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent placing a new point feature on the map at Nairobi, or executing an SQL INSERT statement with Nairobi's coordinates.

task_completion.description: Set the feature name and description attributes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent assigning the name 'Relay_Uplink_Africa' and a description containing 'Nairobi' and its latitude. Because the dataset is missing these columns, look for the agent using a terminal/shell to execute SQLite commands (e.g., ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN) to add 'name' and 'description' before saving the data.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely evaluates the cities rather than using prior knowledge
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should visually inspect the map locations or query the coordinates of the three specified cities before proceeding to Nairobi.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the actual database file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must use a genuine GIS application or a SQLite terminal to modify the .gpkg file, not just write a script without executing it or type answers into a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent properly handles the missing columns
integrity.visual_evidence: If the agent uses a terminal to add the missing columns, verify that the commands are actually executed against the correct world_survey.gpkg database path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target city closest to the equator is Nairobi (latitude ~1.29 degrees South). The target layer is 'field_observations'. The required feature name is 'Relay_Uplink_Africa'. CRITICAL: The 'field_observations' layer in the provided dataset completely lacks the 'name' and 'description' columns. To successfully complete the task, the agent must manually alter the SQLite database schema (e.g., via a terminal using sqlite3) to add these columns before or during the feature creation. If the agent only uses the GUI, it will likely fail to set these specific attributes.
